legal_guarding_position.title: Legal Guarding Position
legal_guarding_position.source_label: NBA Rule No. 12, Personal Fouls — Section II: By Dribbler
legal_guarding_position.summary: To draw a charge, the defender must establish legal guarding position — both feet set, facing the opponent, torso square — before the offensive player begins their upward motion or gathers the ball.
indicators_charge: Defender's feet are set before contact
Defender is facing the offensive player
Defender's torso is square to the ball handler
indicators_block: Defender is still sliding into position at contact
Defender's feet are not set when contact occurs

defender_moving_into_path.title: Defender Moving Into Path
defender_moving_into_path.source_label: NBA Rule No. 12, Personal Fouls — Section I: Types / Section II: By Dribbler
defender_moving_into_path.summary: If the defender is still moving laterally or forward into the offensive player's path at the moment of contact, the defender has not established legal guarding position and a blocking foul may be called.
indicators_charge: Defender stopped all movement before contact
indicators_block: Defender is visibly sliding or lunging at moment of contact
Defender's momentum carries them into the offensive player

airborne_offensive_player.title: Offensive Player Airborne
airborne_offensive_player.source_label: NBA Rule Authority — Block-Charge / Airborne offensive player landing space
airborne_offensive_player.summary: If the offensive player is already airborne (left the ground) before the defender establishes position, the defender cannot draw a charge. A blocking foul may be supported even if the defender appears set at the moment of contact.
indicators_charge: Offensive player initiates contact while still on the ground
indicators_block: Offensive player is already in the air when defender arrives
Defender slides under an airborne player

contact_location.title: Contact Location
contact_location.source_label: NBA Rule No. 12, Personal Fouls — Section I: Types
contact_location.summary: Contact through the defender's torso or chest area may support a charge call if the defender was legally set. Contact initiated by the defender's hip, shoulder, or arm extending into the path may support a blocking foul.
indicators_charge: Contact occurs through the defender's chest or torso
Offensive player drives directly into a set defender
indicators_block: Defender initiates contact with hip or shoulder
Defender leans or extends arm into offensive player's path

restricted_area.title: Restricted Area Note
restricted_area.source_label: NBA Rule Authority — Restricted Area and Block-Charge
restricted_area.summary: Inside the restricted area (the semi-circle under the basket), a defender generally cannot draw a charge on a player driving to the basket. If the play occurs near the restricted area but the court line is not clearly visible, confidence should be lowered.
indicators_charge: Defender is clearly outside the restricted area arc
indicators_block: Defender appears to be inside the restricted area
Restricted area line is not visible — confidence reduced

inconclusive_evidence.title: Inconclusive Evidence
inconclusive_evidence.source_label: NBA Official — Restricted Area Replay Review Guidance
inconclusive_evidence.summary: If the camera angle is poor, feet are blocked from view, video is blurry, or the key moment of contact is not captured in the extracted frames, the analysis should return Inconclusive rather than guessing.
indicators_inconclusive: Key contact moment is not captured
Video quality is too low to assess positioning
